source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - MC 127 A Horn Mountain Platform

context.region: Gulf of America, Mississippi Canyon Block 127
context.operator: Occidental Petroleum (operated for Anadarko Petroleum Corporation)

event.top_event: Firewater system piping rupture and dropped object
actions_taken: Fire Water Pumps A and B started automatically
Jockey Water Pump manually started by Control Room Operator
Fire Water Pumps shut down after piping rupture
Incident reported to BSEE on 26 May 2025
event.summary: On 09 May 2025 at 1530 hours, an Emergency Shutdown event occurred at MC 127 A SPAR platform when EnerMech technicians accidentally initiated a pressure loss in the Temperature Safety Element loop while troubleshooting an air compressor. During recovery from the ESD, multiple procedural failures and equipment issues led to air entering the firewater system. When the Fire Water Pumps were restarted, a water hammer effect ruptured multiple sections of FiberBond firewater piping, causing extensive damage. A metal support clamp weighing 1.5 pounds fell 30 feet from the ruptured piping and landed 5-6 feet from a person near Lifeboat 4. No injuries occurred, but the firewater system was damaged and unable to provide adequate firefighting capability.
recommendations: Update Power Generation System Procedure to include opening SDV 2082 after ESD events
Revise Firewater System Operating Procedure to reference correct valve (PCV 6650) instead of non-existent Sea Chest Valve
Update Power Protection and Life Support Systems Procedures to reflect current JWP equipment
Implement requirement to open helideck hose reels to bleed air from firewater system before starting FWPs
Inspect and repair or replace leaking check valve FSV 6650-004
Review all operating procedures to ensure alignment with current platform design
Add equipment and replace faulty equipment as identified

threats.name: Accidental activation of Bypass Rig Air switch during troubleshooting
Failure to open SDV 2082 valve after ESD event
Leaking check valve FSV 6650-004 in firewater main
Breaker 2E-52 not closed to provide power to JWP
Failure to open helideck hose reels to bleed air from system
Rapid pressure increase when FWPs restarted
Piping support clamp failure under water hammer stress

consequences.name: Firewater piping rupture with multiple leaks and joint failures
Loss of firewater and AFFF capability at helideck and hose reel stations
Dropped object - metal clamp falling 30 feet near personnel
Potential fire hazard due to loss of firefighting capability
Property damage to firewater system components

controls.name: Emergency Shutdown System
controls.barrier_role: Automatic safety system to isolate hazardous conditions
controls.lod_basis: Automatic response to loss of pressure in TSE loop

supporting_text: At 1455 hours, an ESD was initiated in Zone 14 of the platform due to the Temperature Safety Element (TSE) loop losing pressure
the platform muster alarm sound and the platform's flare ignited

controls.name: Automatic Fire Water Pump Start on ESD
controls.barrier_role: Automatic activation of firefighting capability during emergency

supporting_text: Simultaneously, at 1455 hours, the Fire Water Pumps (FWP) A and B along with the Emergency Generator (E-Gen) automatically started, as designed

controls.name: Jockey Water Pump automatic start to maintain pressure
controls.barrier_role: Maintain firewater system pressure during normal operations

supporting_text: the JWP did not automatically start to maintain firewater main pressure due to the 2E-52 Breaker not being closed to power the AMCC 2001 Buss
At 1514 hours, an Occidental platform mechanic discovered that the firewater main had low pressure
the Mechanic contacted the CRO and requested that the JWP be started, if possible

controls.name: Firewater System Operating Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure correct operation of firewater system

supporting_text: the CRO did not follow the Firewater System Operating procedure in place and failed to request the opening of the helideck hose reels to bleed the air from the system prior to starting the FWPs
The firewater hose reels were never opened to allow an escape route for the air that was trapped in the system

controls.name: Procedure for opening SDV 2082 after ESD
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to restore fuel supply to Turbine Generator

supporting_text: SDV 2082 (main diesel outlet valve) was not manually opened after the ESD event occurred. This prevented the startup of the T Gen due to a lack of fuel
This step was not listed in the procedure to reestablish power generation after an ESD event of this type occurs

controls.name: Check valve FSV 6650-004 in firewater main
controls.barrier_role: Prevent backflow and maintain system pressure

supporting_text: pressure to drop, likely due to a leaking check valve (FSV 6650-004). This check valve was stated in Anadarko's internal investigation as having a history of leaks
BSEE believes that pressure was unable to be maintained due to a leaking check valve in the Firewater main system

controls.name: Firewater piping support clamps
controls.barrier_role: Structural support for piping to prevent dropped objects

supporting_text: a metal clamp, weighing approximately 1.5 pounds, that was supporting the piping broke loose and fell to the main deck
The metal clamp fell 30 feet and landed approximately 5-6 feet from an individual who was near the lifeboat

controls.name: FiberBond firewater piping system
controls.barrier_role: Contain and transport firewater under pressure

supporting_text: a water hammer event occurred in the fire water system piping, which is made up of FiberBond piping. The water hammer event caused the piping to rupture in multiple locations
damaged 3 gaskets, created 2 piping joint leaks, 3 piping elbow leaks, 2 T-piping leaks, 3 piping spool leaks, and 1 instance of wear at the spool

controls.name: Training and competence for air compressor operation
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel understand equipment operation to prevent accidental activation

supporting_text: EnerMech not familiar with the operations of the air compressor system installed on the facility
an EnerMech technician accidentally turned the Bypass Rig Air switch on the compressor which caused the TSE system to lose pressure

controls.name: Communication protocol during emergency events
controls.barrier_role: Ensure clear communication between personnel during ESD and recovery operations

supporting_text: there was a lack of communication throughout the ESD event

The Mechanic radioed back to the CRO to confirm and the CRO confirmed the startup. As he was headed to the FWPs he noticed another member of the Occidental maintenance team was already there and had already started the FWPs

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
